message:
feat(policy): replace boolean pressure_is_green with multi-level PressureLevel for decision-theoretic guard scaling

Upgrade PolicyEngine from a binary green/not-green pressure model to the
full PressureLevel enum (Green, Yellow, Orange, Red, Critical), enabling
graduated guard penalty scaling based on decision-theoretic loss asymmetry.

Key changes in policy.rs:
- Replace `pressure_is_green: bool` field with `pressure_level: PressureLevel`
- observe_window() now reads stored pressure_level instead of taking a
  boolean parameter, simplifying the call site in loop_main.rs
- Guard penalty is now scaled by pressure level:
  Green: skip guard entirely (no urgency, no deletions anyway)
  Yellow: full guard penalty (standard behavior)
  Orange: guard penalty x 0.25 (urgency rising, tolerate uncertainty)
  Red/Critical: guard penalty = 0 (bypass guard, survival mode)
- Rationale: disk exhaustion (machine down, all agents killed) vastly
  exceeds the cost of deleting a regenerable build artifact

loop_main.rs: Updated call site to pass PressureLevel instead of bool,
using map_or(PressureLevel::Green) instead of is_none_or().

Test updates (6 files): All tests updated to pass PressureLevel enum
variants instead of boolean values to observe_window() and
set_pressure_level().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
